description_long: This object is a pickaxe-style tool with a long, straight handle and a double-sided, curved metal head. The head features two pointed, symmetrical prongs extending horizontally from a central hub, ideal for breaking hard surfaces. The handle appears to be solid and slightly tapered, providing a comfortable grip for two hands. The silhouette suggests a robust construction, likely intended for heavy-duty use in urban maintenance or construction. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. The tool's proportions and form are consistent with those used for digging or prying in tough ground conditions.
description: A robust pickaxe with a straight handle and a double-pronged, curved metal head, designed for heavy-duty digging or prying.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the curved, double-pronged head and the straight handle beneath.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the head and the handle, with the prongs aligned horizontally.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again showing the full span of the curved head and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the slim profile of the head and handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handle, likely wood, with a slight taper and no visible grip or ergonomic features.
attribute_blade_type: Double-pronged, curved pickaxe head with pointed ends, symmetrical and robust, no visible reinforcement or edge serration.
